Practitioner.title: Practitioner
Practitioner.description: Represents a medical practitioner in the DocTrust application, including their professional details and verification status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Practitioner entity.

name.description: The full name of the practitioner.

specialization.description: The medical specialization of the practitioner (e.g., 'Cardiologist', 'Pediatrician').

medicalRegistrationNumber.description: The unique medical registration number or identifier for the practitioner, used for verification against official lists.

verifiedStatus.description: The current verification status of the practitioner. Possible states: 'UNVERIFIED' (initial state), 'PENDING_REVIEW' (certificate submitted, awaiting check), 'MANUAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED' (automated check failed, needs human review), 'VERIFIED' (successfully verified).

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the practitioner's primary location, used for map display.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the practitioner's primary location, used for map display.

certificateUrl.description: URL to the practitioner's uploaded medical certificate (PDF or image file) for verification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the practitioner's profile was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the practitioner's profile was updated.

definition.description: Stores individual medical practitioner profiles. The `practitionerId` in the path corresponds to the practitioner's Firebase Authentication UID and the `id` field within the document. This collection is publicly readable for patient map/search functionality, and writable only by the owning practitioner. Includes the `verifiedStatus` field for explicit state modeling. The `certificateUrl` field contains the link to their uploaded degree. Includes denormalized 'id' matching 'practitionerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: practitionerId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the practitioner, serving as the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure utilizes a single top-level collection, '/practitioners', to store all medical practitioner profiles. Each document within this collection represents a 'Practitioner' entity, with the document ID (`practitionerId`) being identical to the practitioner's Firebase Authentication UID. This design directly addresses Authorization Independence by ensuring that all authorization context for a practitioner (their ownership and status) is self-contained within their document or derivable from the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules. For example, a practitioner can update their own profile by checking `request.auth.uid == practitionerId`.

This structure also effectively supports QAPs (Queries as Permissions) by allowing public read access to the entire '/practitioners' collection (`allow read: if true;`). This is crucial for the 'Patient Portal' which needs to 'Pull pins from the practitioners collection' to display all doctors on a map and enable filtering by specialization. The client-side application is then responsible for interpreting the `verifiedStatus` field (e.g., showing a blue shield for 'VERIFIED' doctors and a grey dot otherwise) and applying any additional filtering. This approach ensures that security rules grant access to the necessary data without attempting to filter the data, which is an anti-pattern. The `verifiedStatus` field is explicitly modeled as a finite state machine (`UNVERIFIED`, `PENDING_REVIEW`, `MANUAL_REVIEW_REQUIRED`, `VERIFIED`), providing clear intent and debuggability. All documents within '/practitioners' share a homogeneous security posture (publicly readable, owner-writable), aligning with Structural Segregation.
